review_text: Great clean facilities. Lots of space. Jumping pillow, playground and playroom for the kids. Staff were very friendly and allowed early check in and late check out.
We had a cabin it had everything we needed quite bed was comfortable. Easy access
einer der besten Big4 in Tassie
Our family loves coming here. Our dogs love it too. Best location and best staff. Highly recommend.

Great little Caravan Park in a good location.  We stayed in a cabin which had plenty of room for two people.  There was a driveway to park your car right next to your cabin and it had all the amenities you needed.   The only thing I would suggest is a sheer curtain for the windows and sliding glass door.  You either keep the blind open and have no privacy or close it and the cabin is too dark.

I think this is the cleanest , well structured cabin park in Tasmania.
Very helpful staff and a beautiful location

Nice, clean, and well-maintained place overall. I booked a powered concrete/grass site, which was in good condition.

The kitchen facilities are quite well equipped, with crockery, utensils, BBQs, stovetops, toaster, kettles, and a microwave. There’s also a larger lounge sitting area, which is a nice bonus. The whole campground was quiet and relaxing, especially considering it wasn’t fully booked.

Amenities were generally clean, although two of the shower units were out of order. A nice touch was the addition of shower foot towels, even though we didn’t end up using them.

My main complaint is that there were no cloths or wipes available in the kitchen to clean surfaces after use. This wasn’t helped by some guests not cleaning up after themselves, leaving the kitchen area untidy at times.

Overall, a pleasant stay with good facilities, just a few small improvements needed.
Good spot, near city center.

Stayed here for 2 nights but extended our stay for an extra three nights. It wasn't that the park was fantastic and we wanted to stay there, it was because we had intended to go to swimcart beach camping but the weather was so poor not beach weather we decided to stay longer at the park.
The park was well maintained and the ablutions were clean. We had site 15 right by the laundry which was quite noisy from opening to closing. It was a tight fit getting in and out packed in roads too narrow.
There was a bike/walking path just opposite the park so it was easy to get to the pier and into town.
Would be good if they could put the arrival time for sites on the email so no confusion. Nobody expects to have to wait until 2pm if on a site.
Great park in a great spot, will definitely return.

Would have preferred to have given 3 1/2 stars if it was possible. Stayed in a superior cabin and while it was fine (apart from a slightly dodgy shower) it was not a particularly pleasant place to stay. Too much concrete, a view of a cabin wall through the sliding doors and a lounge window that looked straight into the next cabin so we kept the blinds down most of the time. Could have been excellent but not enough thought put into how people experience cabin life.
The unpowered sites are on a hill so it’s impossible to set up on a flat. As one of the more expensive sites jn Tas, this was disappointing. The hot showers take the score from 2 to 3 stars.

Amazing caravan park. Many facilities for kids, the amenities are very well kept.
We even got pancakes on our departure.
Laundry is 5 bucks per load and same for the drier.
Also conveniently located to the center of town. We will certainly come back .
an amazing park and such lovely staff…booked a cabin and received an upgrade…everything is so clean and tidy…and a beautiful town

What a great park. We stayed in a double cabin, the inclusions were great, everything you need to be self contained. Excellent laundry and plenty for the kids to do as well. Staff were super friendly and St Helens offered lots of recreational activities. I can’t wait to return here.

The staff are friendly and efficient and park is clean. We had a powered evergreen site which was relatively level and well maintained. Only downsides were that the site we had was in front of permanent vans and there was limited space to back your van, plus some permanent residents feed the seagulls, so there was some bird poo on our car & van. The park is close to the town centre and drives to Akaroa and Binalong Bay, as well as heading to St Mary’s and west to Campbell Town/Ross. Overall would recommend this park.
It's a typical caravan park..huge facility and very near to waters and shops ...very relaxing place ...but the bathroom got limited place for clothes or mo cooks to put the clothes

Great staff and large sites to the bigger caravans, lots of amenities close by within the location itself. Close to town with supermarkets with lots of dinner options. Close to beaches and local sites. Highly recommend.

Lovely home away from home
experience.
Very clean and neat park.
Extremely lovely and helpful staff.

We arrived at this beautiful caravan park, the facilities are brilliant, the camp kitchen the bbq area stunning. Unfortunately there is only 5 showers for a large park for both men and women. 2 of the showers were not working in both men and women’s showers, I know sometimes this is beyond control butttt

Lovely caravan park with grassy sites and lots of activities for kids.
Love that they supply "foot towels" for the showers which is like a floor mat to get dry on.
Lovely camping ! Power sites are big enough, everything is clean and a lot of amenities. Perfect ! And wallabies if you’re lucky..
Last minute unplanned stay in our camper van. The staff were very polite, friendly and helpful. The park is very clean and well laid out. Great location. Highly recommend.

Localisation vraiment top, et la chambre enfants est bien pensée avec 4 lits, donc sur le papier parfait pour une famille nombreuse. Par contre, gros point noir : la table est beaucoup trop petite pour manger à 6, et la cuisine n’est pas adaptée au quotidien (pas assez de rangements pour stocker la nourriture). Dommage, car avec un meilleur aménagement ce serait un sans-faute.

Walking distance to town, friendly receptionists, maintenance was required for blocked overflowing toilets/blocked sinks, but clean and tidy amenities. Cheap washing machines/dryer and ample clothes lines for all families. I Didn't book a room but the cabins looked cleaned and tidy and present nicely.
Staff were very helpful. Went out of there way to come and help us after we locked ourselves out of the room at night.
Great location, service, price & accomodation. Ill be back.
Stayed in the bunkhouse with our 2 dogs. Shared facilities were clean and well maintained, well above expectations. Reception staff very friendly.

Mixed experience; the pros: evergreen sites are nice and level, and plenty spacious. Camp kitchen clean and well equipped. Complientary foot towels are a nice touch. Cons: evergreen sites are on the only unpaved road on-site which creates a lot of dust. It doesn't help that noone follows the 5 km/h speed limit along it either. Bumps and more signals might help. Camp kitchen is not centrally located so can be a bit of a walk. Some "sites" being used while we were there probably shouldn't be - no 1 is a case in point right backing right on to amenities block and facing jumping pillow - best avoided. Unpowered camping area at the top of the park is highly sloped- also best avoided. It's a decent location for access to waterfront and St Helens, but expensive for what it is.

Absolutely fantastic park- sets a new high bar. Staff are lovely but the true standout is the feel of the park. Our youngest said she wanted to move in- people staying all seemed to be warm and friendly and welcoming. The price for powered sites is very reasonable.
Camp kitchen is huge with its own bathrooms and attached tv room for kids. Also has a roofed area with pizza ovens which we’ll try next time.
Games room has arcade machines and a baby soft play area plus a tv and loads of books for all ages of children.
Bouncing pad is from 8am to dusk and huge- big kids would jump on one side so littles could fly high - lots of fun.
Only suggestions to make it even better- credit card facilities for laundry- who carries cash these days?! On the other hand I did have lots of conversations while walking around seeing if anyone could change my note. Also recycling bins - I really really don’t like binning bottles, cans and cardboard. Regardless we’re planning our next stay!

Great facilities but awfully expensive for a level site with power etc. I had a non powered site which wasn’t even levelled either, with no services and it was $50 a night. Levelled sites with power and water were $90 a night. Ridiculous

A lovely park - very well maintained and clean
Friendly staff and fantastic facilities
Loved the gold coin breakfast pancakes put on by the staff with all proceeds going to charity
What a great idea!

Don't go anywhere near the unpowered sites in busy times. More uneven sloping ground than there is level and it's first in best dressed.  But everyone pays the same price . Dogs are allowed.  Lots and lots of dogs. What a show !
Clean facilities, great new bbq area out the back surrounded by nature.

Very clean right through the cabin. Customer service fantastic. Loved staying 4 nights before driving to Devonport Spirit of Tasmania. The IGA groceries fresh stock daily. Very clean, very friendly customer service & the local very friendly. Affordable & excellent prices for Pensions & members of Big4.  This is a must to stay in when on holiday
Great staff. Well maintained

We had a powered site. The park is lovely.
Very big, pet friendly and staff are wonderful.

Stayed on a powered site. A great caravan park. The best I’ve stayed in in Tasmania. Friendly and helpful staff. Beautifully kept grounds. Amenities are modern and clean . Camp kitchen is well equipped, big with toilets.
Sites are a good size and easy to manoeuvre into. Good value for money. Highly recommend.
stayed 3 nights in caravan. Good, clean place, handy camp kitchen facilities, friendly and helpful staff
Really well done. Camp kitchen is a ripper. Enjoy.

Our stay at the big4 has always been delightful 🥰 this time we stayed in one of the studio cabins very clean and comfortable lots of things to do complimentary tea and coffee very thoughtful touches throughout the cabin. This is our place to stay for a relaxing getaway and we can bring our French bulldog Alfie who also enjoyed the scenery see you all next time ❤️

This is such a pretty little park in a convenient place. The beauty of the area is reflected in the wonderful presentation of the cabins. I greatly enjoyed my time here. It was a very pleasant break between long windy roads.

We spent quite a bit of time around our cabin reading on the comfortable furniture or enjoying the weather on the decking.

The staff were polite and helpful though I had little interaction with them. I appreciate the sweet gift for Big4 members.

The location is about 1km outside of the centre of town. The walk is beautiful along the waterfront which is also a bike trail.
Plenty of great food options in town and supermarkets.
The cabins are nice, the bedrooms were small but everything is clean and comfortable.
Great camp kitchen,  pizza oven, cooking facilities etc.
Would definitely stay here, in St Helens rather than the surrounding areas as I felt there was more to do without having to drive.
Had a great relaxing week  staying at the Big4 Caravan Park why we explored the great East Coast. Great spot to stay, neat and tidy facilities.
Toller Park mit Hütten und Stellplätzen, manche haben sogar ein eigenes Badezimmer dabei. Der Standort ist OK,bca. 1 km einen schönen Spazierweg am Wasser entlang, dann ist man in der Stadt.
Sehr schöner Platz in der Nähe vom Zentrum. Duschen und Toiletten sehr sauber
Nice
We had a great stay. Love the playground and games room. Pizza oven and kitchen is awesome. Staff were friendly .

We had a great stay, the rooms are modern and the bed was very comfortable. Parking for large vehicles are next to each cabin and the kitchen came fully stocked with all the accessories we needed and was very clean. The park is a haven for small kids and the staff were very good with their local knowledge. While our stay was short it was enjoyable and peaceful and we would have no hesitation in recommending them.
Walkable distance to the cosy town centre

Great place to stay! I went with my campervan, and we made pizza in the wood-fired oven and enjoyed a fire pit while stargazing.
Hot water, clean toilets and showers, a TV room, and full kitchen facilities. Overall, an excellent place!
Love this place.  Our preferred stop over in St Helens

Nicely kept at the right price.
I'll be back.
Love the big 4 experience

Had problems with some permanents who thought they were the dog police, apart from that, great facilities, showers are second to none, the place is spotless, great staff and reasonable prices for powered site, plenty of options..🩷😊

Fantastic caravan park with all internal roads being sealed, enclosed kitchen and entertainment rooms. Amenities were very tidy. We parked our campervan on beautiful green grass and were even allowed to have a fire (in own firepit). Dogs are welcome. If you like it neat, tidy and clean, then this is a great place for you.

Not a campsite I would recommend, especially for unpowered site campers.
The showers had very weak water pressure — something that should’ve taken just a few minutes ended up taking twice as long, and the experience was quite uncomfortable. The shower space was also small and cramped, with only five stalls in a single shared area.

The unpowered sites were poorly maintained. The ground didn’t drain well and was mostly sand, which stayed wet and muddy after rain. It felt like the campground wasn’t designed with unpowered sites in mind.

Toilets and other facilities weren’t cleaned frequently enough — every time I used them, they were quite dirty.

Update

Good thing I’m the only one who pointed out how dirty the toilets were — or your rating might not be this high

This really is a 5 star holiday park. Facilities are spot on and grounds are immaculate. Staff are so friendly.  Big shout to Nicole ,Rebecca and Jason.
Thanks for a lovely stay.
We enjoyed a small splash of luxury mid trip with the premium en-suite. Lovely to enjoy a private shower and toilet along with a few drinks and dinner around the fire pit 👌🏻

This Big4 is nice and close to the town centre if you want to walk. Park has a games room for the kids and / or big kids. There is a playground, but it is a bet of a letdown compared to mainland Big4 for the same price per night. Our grassed power site was large, well maintained, and opposite the playground.
Free activities for the kids during the Easter break were great.
Jumping pillows were the most popular activity for the kids, I think, mainly because of the uninteresting playground.
We tested out the woodfired pizza oven, which worked great, but make sure you plan early as it takes about 45-50 minutes to heat up.
The communal kitchen facilities are spacious and well stocked.
Showers are a little on the small side, which is not great if you have kids with you and look a bit tired. Clothing hooks were missing in some, and those old school shower heads that can't be adjusted properly or fall down when you try because they have worn out.
Also, it is an option to bring a dog as well.
Overall, it's not a bad Big4, but I've been in better ones for the same money.
글램핑은 호주에서 최고의 시설이엿다

Absolutely 5 star. Really worth the stay. Had a powered site with ensuite- lovely! Facilities are awesome with change available for laundrette in the recreational room via your mastercard/debit card etc. Everything is so clean and tidy. A new jumping pillow for the kids was being installed when I arrived. Restaurant is open for lunch and dinner, really good honest food. Service was excellent. Great advice regards driving/road conditions to other locations. 10/10 would stay again in a heartbeat.
Awesome place great staff really enjoyed it

Stayed here with a camper, easy people to deal with powered site decent ammenities
If your travelling and need a site for your camper I would recommend big4 st Helen’s, what a great place to visit on your Tassie trip

Very good place to go with your motorhome/caravan, etc.
Facilities and amenities comfortables and well maintained...
Everything is really clean and neat.
The staff is specially friendly people, always willing to help you.
5 stars for the reception desk workers.
Highly recommendable. Thank you very much.
Very well presented cabins. Very clean and tidy, inside and out. A bit of a walk into town, but a very nice walk along the waterfront.
Great tourist park
Stayed overnight at the camper van parking (powered). Clean toilets and showers, great location, and friendly staff. Overall, a pleasant experience.
Great park fantastic array of ammenities plenty for all ages to do

We stayed on an ensuite site which provided ample space to set up and park our vehicle, the ensuite itself was presented extremely well and was also spacious.

The park facilities we utilised which were the indoor and outdoor camp kitchen, lounge room and fire pit area are all outstanding and well appointed.

The friendly staff held a happy hour at the fire pit area on the Friday night with nibbbles supplied.
It's a lovely and easy walk into the town centre, where we found all the shops we needed and were surprised to find a combined cinema, brewery & restaurant called Easy Tiger where we spent one evening.

Thank you for a great stay BIG4 Tassie Getaway Parks St Helens and St Helens, we will recommend you to all.
Excellent park, friendly staff and great quality service.

We visited here in September 2024. This was our first glamping experience and it was so much fun. The design of glamping area was well thought out with lots of privacy and quietness.

The glamping tent itself was very clean and well stocked. The outside bathtub was fun and loved the firepit to sit by at night.

The whole park was lovely and the staff very helpful.

We would highly recommend this park and the glamping is a must try.

Thanks for a great experience.
We had a very small cabin. Allegedly for 4 people but even 2 was crowded.
Our little dog gave the cabin a 4 paws of approval as well.🐾🐾👍🌹

Nice new cabins. Comfortable and tidy.
Pleasant experience.

Superb park. We had booked a powered site online. But our camper trailer needed repairs at a local mechanic. We were able to change our booking to a cabin without penalty and just pay the difference. They also found us the cheapest option then upgraded us to a better cabin.

Our cabin was wonderful. We had a balcony looking out at big gum trees. It was a new cabin with comfortable beds and excellent amenities. The park laundry was excellent and reasonably priced.

They have boat/trailer parking so once our camper trailer was fixed, we could park it safely onsite not far from our cabin.

Definitely recommend
A beautifully maintained, conveniently located caravan park run by caring staff. We enjoyed it so much we extended our stay.

Stayed 2 nights on a caravan site. The kids loved the games room & jumping pillow. Sites were tidy, well marked & easy to get into. Amenities were always clean & showers were roomy. Thanks for a great stay.

An excellent campsite with plentiful clean and well maintained amenities, a great camp kitchen, lounge and BBQ area. The ecco glamping area is a fantastic initiative but it is let down by the fact that I could not find a refuse bin for recyclable waste.

My 8 year old and I booked the bunk house room and it was as expected. The entire place - kitchen, toilets, bathrooms were kept super clean. I noticed the staff were doing regular cleaning throughout the day.

We spent two nights and I recommend this place.

We stayed in a six-guest cabin for one night, expecting it to comfortably accommodate four adults. Unfortunately, the cabin was poorly configured. There were no proper bath essentials provided (only two small soaps) and needed to access the bathroom by passing through one of the sleeping areas. The cabin had no living room area, only one power socket inconveniently placed near the ceiling, and the overall space was very cramped. Adding curtains to separate the sleeping areas instead of a desk would be a practical improvement. Additionally, the park is overcrowded with cabins and caravan/camping areas, making for an unpleasant and noisy overnight stay. Compared to other holiday parks we stayed at during our 4-day trip, which charged similar prices, the quality here was significantly lower. Not recommended for value for money. Average customer service.

Rooms floor dirty
Can’t connect to wifi.

A great location with a wide variety of facilities, quiet at night too. It is the first facility of its type we have visited over many years where lots of people drive fast and there are many young children around. There are no speed signs posted anywhere. Some speed signs and common sense by drivers more so may save someone getting hurt.
Good spot close to town . Cabins are good

The pros-
Park playground, jumping pillow and indoor play area are great.
Toilets and shower very clean great water pressure in shower.

The cons-
Dog poo everywhere on the powered grass sites at the front. There is a sign saying its kangaroos but its not. I literally watched a little dog come down one morning and shit a meter from our site so I dare say its a residents dog. I have a crawling 8 month old so probably paid more attention then most but it is everywhere and very easy to tell the difference between that and wallaby or kangaroo poo which is also present. I noted this was a complaint in previous reviews so obviously the park isnt bothered by it and allows it to continue. I didnt think I would be paying to pick up dog poo.

Majority of the washers are out of order and we paid $16 to dry our clothes thats 4 goes in the working dryer. I would have not bothered after the second but I was time poor and needed the kids clothes dried.

With the amount of free camps available in the surrounding areas I would have thought the washing facilities and grounds would be kept in better condition.

Nice location HOWEVER  the facilities were not cleaned. Facilities include separate toilets, shower blocks  and all camp kitchen and BBQ areas. We were there a Saturday, Sunday. Then try and watch TV in camp kitchen when everyone rightfully so are cooking meals. Yes there is a TV room off the kitchen however no door so still can not enjoy. Then stay in inpowered  site and bring in your power board and charge all in camp kitchen.Find it hard to see why they get awards when as a Big4 park very disappointing.  Lastly watch people drive in and use Facilities and drive out no codes required.

Stayed in a cabin for 2 nights on our east coast Tassie trip. Friendly staff and good location but the facilities and cabin need some work. Half the washing machines in the laundry were out of order. Cabin was cramped with some lights broken and showerhead needs replacing. Was ok but can’t help but think we would have been better to stay at the newer holiday park down the road.
Great place for the budget conscious. Cabins of varying size and cost. Caravan and camper sites available. Free Wifi is available but a little weak in places.
Well kept, clean amenities, good water pressure, great staff. Very friendly, very impressed

Very clean cabins, however lacking storage with no wardrobe. Shower head needed descaling or replacing.  Everything else was awesome.  All staff are friendly while being professional and very approachable. Amenities are in good order except for half of washing machines and dryers being out of order

We recently had a fantastic stay at the luxury ensuite site at St Helens BIG4 Holiday Park. The ensuite was spotless and well-equipped, adding a real touch of comfort to our camping experience. The facilities were top-notch, and the park was beautifully maintained, creating a relaxing and inviting atmosphere. The staff were friendly and always eager to help, which made us feel right at home. The location is perfect for exploring the stunning Bay of Fires and other local attractions. We highly recommend this park for anyone looking for a bit of luxury in the heart of nature. Can't wait to come back ☺️

The Park is beautifull and clean all round except #1. Dog poo (fresh and not so fresh) all around our site while parked up next to the amenities... people obviously don't feel like picking it up.
#2. Lady at the counter didn't really care about our complaint and didn't apologise which we thought wasn't very professional.

Great park and we had a great time away. Sadly the wood-fire pizza oven was out of action (let’s hope it returns) and we arrived earlier than we had planned but at 2pm our room still wasn’t ready to check in which wasn’t the best.

We came here after office hour and can still got checked in. While somewhere else will refuse us to do a late check in. The place is nice, they have a playground for child to play. We selected a paid campground, done our laundry and we had a good hot shower there. We paid our campsite in the next morning and the reception also nice and helpful.
The location is near to the shopping center in St. Helens.
Awesome spot to stay, everything in the park was great. We liked the camp kitchen the most. .

Stayed on the Luxury ensuite in a motorhome.
I’ve stayed in many parks & haven’t seen an ensuite site that even comes close!!
Lovely claw-foot bath & shower with awesome pressure.
This site even has its own fire pit area with string lights all above.
Girls on reception were friendly & helpful. The whole park is clean & a credit to the grounds staff.
Has a new glamping area which looks amazing (we’ll have to try them out next visit).
Thanks again…we’ll definitely be back 👍

Nice clean room but had trouble with ants all over the kitchen sink,stove etc.
They got into food pkts etc.
Amenities a 10
Firepit 10
Pizza oven and BBQ 10
Thank you for a lovely stay
We had a really nice stay here with our two children. Lots of fun things to do including the jumping pillow, games room and walks.
Good value 3nights $90

Originally booked an unpowered site for self sufficient tent camping and wae told it was a nice spacious area. Upon arrival the unpowered section was absolute rubbish. Mostly dirt, tree roots and on a decent slope. Probably only one or two decent areas where you might consider setting up. Went back to the office and conveyed my feelings and was upgraded to a nice grassy powered site near the amenities which was fantastic. Don't like complaining to office staff generally speaking, but frankly I can't believe they offer that patch of otherwise useless land as a camp area.

Otherwise speaking, great neat park with great amenities even offering bath mats for the showers which is a nice touch. Great camp kitchen and facilities.

Would stay again but either up your game and do something to tidy up your unpowered area, or just don't offer it for use at all.
Very well maintained caravan park and plus points for the staffs, Cheers!
A great place to go camping with the family.
Would be wonderful to have a bidet sprayer installed in the toilets for easy cleaning 😉
Great park and location,  big sites, not the cheapest but the best.
Great location and Amenities
All the staff were polite. Our cabin was very nice, clean and had good cooking utensils and facilities. We would stay here again on our next visit.
Great place to go for a weekend
Nice caravan park close to town. Modern facilities and friendly staff.
We enjoyed an unpowered site which was close to a sensational kitchen that was modern & clean. Very reasonably  priced with friendly staff. 20 minute coastal walk into town.

Stay in the glamping tents, it's an experience all on it's own. We loved it so much, we had plans for dinner out but just got something easy to have at the tent/cabin to make the most of time there! Luxe experience with fun little touches - thank you!
Very nice place has nice outdoor kitchen games area for the kids
Best cabin we stayed in Tasmania.
A bit daunted when I found out we were in a bunkhouse container. Should not have worried. Aircon great, beds good. Bathroom facilities very close and clean. Overall excellent.
This is an exvetcaravan park with top amenities, good size sites and very clean. No pool but that is  only a small matter

Well after visiting and using the glamping accommodation with a group of mates,  the Mrs spotted the pics and demanded we go on holidays there, as per my 1st visit,  absolutely awesome,  Mrs enjoyed and was comfortable,  whipped up a platter and enjoyed around the fire , thanks for the great stay.
Great amenities

We stayed in the bunkhouse.
The facilities were fantastic.
Everything you could need, even a pizza oven.

Absolutely lovely place.
Staff were extremely helpful and friendly and more than happy to accommodate.
Grounds and amenities were tidy and clean and well maintained.
We would definitely go back and stay again and would recommend it to others.

We had an ensuite camp site which was clean & pleasant.  Staff were VERY friendly.  Large camp kitchen & dining room available.  It's just slightly out of town, a 2-minute drive across the beautiful bay to shops & restaurants.
Good cheap campsites with all the facilities
One of the best holiday parks...extremely amaculate place to stay..would recommend 👌
Expensive.
Great staff, lovely facilities and location.

Great things:
Was pleasantly surprised to receive an upgrade to their new 2 bed cabins on arrival. The new cabins are great. Super comfy beds and linen, fridge and full kitchen set ups. Also loved the Nespresso machine and milk frother! The service from reception was also very welcoming and lovely.

Could improve:
As someone else has recently posted a similar thing, I thought there would be a fair bit more to do with a 2 year old boy there, but unfortunately there wasn’t. The playground is old and not suitable for little kids, and it’s quite a small area. My toddler wasn’t interested in the indoor centre at all, and the jumping pillow usually had bigger kids on it so he wasn’t keen on that either. We did bring his little balance bike to ride thinking the park would be on fairly level ground, but unfortunately it’s quite sloped so wasn’t safe for him to ride in most areas. There is a good pathway just off the caravan path that leads right around the bay and into town which is lovely. Would be great to have more green and garden spaces and a pool too.

Overall a great stay though!

We only stayed in the unpowered site area. But very little attention is paid to that area. The flat bits are gravelly and the grassy parts are on an angle, so not suited to tent camping. Having said that, the facilities were modern and clean.
Camped here in a non powered site over Christmas.  Great park in a great location, with easy access to St Helens and the coastline. Note to other campers, the park is on a hill and not flat.

Having saught this campsite out to hide from the weather we were reluctant to pay the 78$ for two nights in a tent. Although having to pay this much we expected the tent sites be either flat grass or at least ground soft enough to drive our pegs through without bending them due to the rock hard patch we were given. Not only this but most of the ammenities were out of order and the cherry on top was the wifi they offered didnt even reach the tent site. Of all the camp sites we stayed at in tassie, most free and the middle of nowhere this one was by far the most expensive and overall the worst. Ill be steering people away from this caravan park in the future.

Edit: Because I cant reply to your message, i think it's a cop out to say its first in best dressed because regardless if youre first or last youre two camping options are either ground so hard you need a serious hammer to drive the pegs or if you choose the grass youre on quite a slope making that just as annoying. I think you guys just happened to have some space left over and decided to get some more money by offering "unpowered tent sites" which are terrible.

We stayed in the MTB bunkhouse accommodation. Great value. Funky shipping containers. Modern, clean, tidy, great native gardens, fire pit, great well equipped kitchen and outdoor eating area. Pizza oven that is wood fired. Wood provided for both fire pit and wood fired pizza oven.

What a splendid experience and stay. My first time in a bunkhouse . . What a great creative, beautiful and comfortable concept! Well done on getting this absolutely right, with no compromise on service!

The park was ok but needs some improvements. Firstly there is nothing for younger kids to do. The playground that is there is far too big for smaller children around the age of 1-2 and although there is an indoor play centre in literally only had foam blocks in it for younger kids to play with - its in desperate need of an upgrade - and of course the jumping pillow is also far too big for younger children to play on especially with big kids on it all day. A pool would also be a great addition!!

I had an unpowered camp site & @ $35 for the night was very expensive given the inconvenience of access to the toilets & showers, as well as the camp kitchen. Fortunately I got a fairly flat site, but the majority of the "camping area" was sloping land & your campers are treated like second rate customers, having the furthest distance to any facilities on your park....not even a tap in the camping area. Very poor!

Booked a powered caravan site and a cabin last January for this December at a premium price with a request for us to be close together. However when we arrived the cabin was a two bedroom (on my own?) and couldn't have been further apart. The usual buck passing happened but couldn't be changed because they were fully booked. The caravan had to be raised quite a lot on one side also. Maybe some levelling wouldn't go astray? I would suggest anyone booking here make sure the I's are dotted and the T's crossed and get a site map! Disappointing start to our holiday. Having said all that, the kids really enjoyed their park experience especially the jumping pillow.
Nice caravan park. Great spot with my mobile home.

I cant rate this park highly enough. It’s no surprise it has won awards recently. The ladies in reception are all so lovely and welcoming and the park amenities are well maintained and clean.

Perfect location for walking into town and great little foreshore bike ride too. Looking forward to our next stay VERY soon. Great job to the team at Big4 St Helens

One of the very best caravan parks we stayed in while touring Tasmania. We had a luxury en-suite powered site with a private fire pit. Everything about this caravan park is excellent from time of visit to departure. Managers and caretakers are superb very friendly, helpfull and courteous. It's definitely worth the few extra dollars per night.
Good Camp site's

Our accomodation exceeded expectations. The facilities were well organised, clean and tidy.
Booking was seamless and the reception staff Lily and Emily are a true testament to the fantastic quality of the Big 4's team. Myself and my travel buddy would recommend this place to any lone adventurer or a travelling family. Thanks a bunch :)

Easy Check in,  beautiful location , very well maintained park. Accommodation everything you need. Parkside Restuarant fabulous staff. Highly recommend
Thank you for an enjoyable stay
Great caravan park, within walking distance of town, near the water. Great caravan spot, beautiful cabins

A big THANK YOU to Lily and team for your honesty and help last week when my friend left behind their cash and medicine upon checking out. Also the flexibility to allow another friend to change to cabin from their glamping tents booked. Your excellence customer service definitely lock us in at your campsite every time we visit St Helen. Will be back again soon!
Very nice park, with good facilities and a great location; walking distance to the town centre.
Good spot. Large facilities which are decent.

Nice clean park. Great facilities. Currently under renovation. Camp kitchen is way at the back of the property but bathrooms are towards the front. Probably needed another of each for the size of the place.

Came to St Helens for a business meeting. Perfect cabin, lovely and inviting management team.
Could not be happier with the accommodation, unfortunately I am only here for 2 nights, but 150% shall return.

We were passing through. Seeing as it was winter  we had a pick of the park for our camper.
The ammenities were clean and well presented.
Close to town.
$70 for an ensuite site in the off season is a little step.
Great big four has an entertainment room for the kids jumping pillow great camp kitchen person ensuite to camp beside can have a camp fire great stay

I stayed in the budget twin room, which I found quite cosy and clean! The room stayed quite warm and had air conditioning which worked a treat.
We had shared bathrooms for this accommodations which were great, except the water from the shower would leak all over the floor. There was a shower curtain, however it didn’t do much. They were cleaned daily though and if you got in quick, you got a dry floor.
The shared space was quite nice, and there were pancakes for breakfast!
The only complaint I have, is that since the accommodation was built in a shipping container, whenever we closed the door, we had no service or wifi. So would not recommend if you’re travelling for work!

Such great facilities, we loved having access to the clean camp kitchen, bathrooms and the fire pit on the cold nights! It was a great easy dinner idea to have pizza packs (for sale) for the wood fire pizza oven on site.
